rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ERNESTO JAUREGUI SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #11, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/28/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). 2) The trustee is requesting $1,00,1462.12 in fees. Attached to the petition is a fee schedule with annual fees set higher than 1%, the amount this court allows per annum of the fair market value of assets at the end of the accounting period (LRSF 14.52). No calculation of said fees is provided, and no time records are attached. In a verified supplement, please provide the calculation for said fees, and please provide the factors justifying said fees as set forth in CRC 7.776. Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 07/14/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARNOLD YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/18/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The fourth amended petition was e-filed on 6/1/26. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] "Filing" means when the pleading requiring notice appears on the Court's register of actions (and becomes publicly viewable in decedent estate and trust cases). (It is unclear who all the notice recipients are, besides the prior personal representative and her attorney, and why they were given notice.) (2) A petition for final distribution must also state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] (3) If this accounting begins on 1/17/25, assets on hand should not be the I&A (date of death) values. The former administrator's accounting showed a balance of $2,381,317.75 as of 1/16/25. (4) The ending date appears to be 4/17/26, not the "completion of administration." (5) Paragraph 14 shows "additional receipts" of $12,294.74, but the accounting alleges, and statutory fee calculation shows, $44,823.95 in receipts. (6) The apportioned statutory compensation figures shown in paragraphs 15 and 16 of the fourth amended petition do not agree with the amounts shown on Schedule K (Liabilities) of the accounting. (7) Some of the Schedule K math appears to be incorrect. (8) Schedule K also shows an "extraordinary fees reserve" of $15,000, although the petition shows an "additional extraordinary attorney's fee" in the amount of $9,960.00. Please provide a courtesy copy of any new filed papers to the assigned examiner by 8/7/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. ( "Amended pleading" means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3(3).] It should stand on its own and contain NO reference to prior filings or issues raised by the examiner.) NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] After all issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required in connection with the request for extraordinary attorney fees.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/22/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. There was just one FINAL I&A filed on 6/30/25, not a "Corrected Final" I&A filed on 5/19/25. Petitioner is reported to be Decedent's sister, so references to "his" report or "his" sureties is presumably erroneous.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VIVIAN L MACK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. 1. The will names Ronald A. Mack as one of Decedent's two living children. The petition lists Ronald L. Mack as the now pre-deceased son. 2. The proof of service filed on 5/21/26 showed "Amended" in the caption of the NOTICE, not Notice of AMENDED PETITION or in item 2, that "An Amended Petition... has been filed," etc. Similarly, the proof of publication filed on 5/26/26 only shows "Amended" in the title, not in the body of the notice. 3. A copy of the trust pages showing that Reginald O. Mack and Doris Jackson were named as the co-trustees has not been filed; the will names the person(s) serving as trustee(s) to be "executor." 4. Only one page of the trust was attached to the attorney's declaration filed on 3/11/26, showing that the "net proceeds... remaining after compliance with the previous provisions shall be distributed" to Reginald O. Mack (or Doris Jackson, had he not survived). Petitioner has not disclosed what the "previous provisions" are. (There was no need to "declare" that a new notice would be published; the correct notice just needed to be published and proof of publication filed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YU AN YANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. It appears that the decedent's interest in the real property (50%) is her separate property. Her parents' written consent to characterize it as community property is not appropriate, as property characterization is not determined by consent. The Court may confirm that only half of the separate property interest (25%) passes to the surviving spouse, while the other half does not. The petition should state the legal authority that would allow the Court to determine that the parents' 25% share passes to the surviving spouse without administration, while Section 13501(a) expressly states that this 25% is subject to administration. The facts in #308589 seem different. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN STANLEY PHILLIPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues: 1) The DE-121 form filed on 06/05/2026 did not state that a "Third Amended" (Petition for Probate) has been filed (words "Third Amended" were not added to item 2 of the form). 2) The proof of publication filed on 06/10/2026 did not identify who filed the third amended petition. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES R. COST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/7/26. A Court-appointed fiduciary or a person seeking such appointment must supplement or amend her own pleading(s), not the attorney. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] The petitioner's verified supplement must be filed to provide the missing information in item 5a(3) or (4) of her petition. Please provide the assigned examiner a courtesy copy of the petitioner's supplement by 6/30/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A proper response to issues posted by an examiner is either a SUPPLEMENT that provides additional information or an AMENDMENT that corrects allegations (or a fully amended petition), not a "declaration" narrating how the defects will be cured. [CRC 7.3.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARCIA PATRICIA JULIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. MICHAEL KASOLAS will be appointed probate referee. DISCLOSURE: The assigned probate referee is married to a legal advisory staff member of this court. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 01/11/2028.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH ANN TOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Matter will be called to admit will. No appearance is required. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 12/28/27. No appearance will be necessary on that date if either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Probate Code 12200 et seq is filed at least two weeks prior.

rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit will. No appearance required. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 01/11/2028.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAYMOND L. NG

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/7/26 at Counsel's request. The waivers of bond were incomplete at item 4. The Receipt of Acknowledgment of the Duties and Liabilities has the wrong address and phone number for the petitioner.
